rationale: In the citing paper, Ding, Grewal, and Liechty (2005) is cited only as an example supporting a general point about conjoint validation practice, namely using aggregate preference share predictions with holdout stimuli. The citation does not play a distinct role in the PCPM framework, hypothesis development, or core method comparisons. There is no direct reuse or discussion of the cited paper's incentive-alignment mechanism or findings.
